dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7149 IH: Veteran Housing Promise Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Riley of New York introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Veterans' Affairs

form.official-title: To amend title 38, United States Code, to eliminate the maximum authorizations of appropriations for certain benefits for homeless veterans administered by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs and to make permanent the authority of the Secretary to carry out certain programs for homeless veterans.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Veteran Housing Promise Act.

section.header: Elimination of maximum authorizations of appropriations for certain benefits for homeless veterans

subsection.text: Section 2016 of title 38, United States Code, is amended to read as follows:

section.text: There is authorized to be appropriated such sums as may be necessary to carry out this subchapter.

subsection.text: Section 2021(i)(1) of title 38, United States Code, is amended—

paragraph.text: in subparagraph (G), by striking fiscal year 2024 and each fiscal year thereafter and inserting each of fiscal years 2024 through 2026; and
by adding at the end the following new subparagraph:

subparagraph.text: For fiscal year 2027 and each subsequent fiscal year, such sums as may be necessary.

subsection.header: Homeless women veterans and homeless veterans with children reintegration grant program

paragraph.text: by striking section $1,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2011 through 2025 and inserting section—; and
by adding at the end the following new paragraphs:
$1,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2011 through 2025; and
for fiscal year 2026 and each subsequent fiscal year, such sums as may be necessary.

subsection.header: Financial assistance for supportive services for very low-Income veteran families in permanent housing
subsection.text: Section 2044(e) of title 38, United States Code, is amended to read as follows:
From amounts appropriated to the Department for Medical Services, there shall be available such sums as may be necessary to carry out subsections (a), (b), and (c).

subsection.header: Grant program for homeless veterans with special needs
subsection.text: Section 2061(d) of title 38, United States Code, is amended—

paragraph.text: by striking for each of fiscal years 2007 through 2025, $5,000,000 shall be available for each such fiscal year for the purposes of the program under this section. and inserting amounts shall be available for the purposes of the program under this section as follows:; and
by adding at the end the following new paragraphs:
For each of fiscal years 2007 through 2025, $5,000,000.
For fiscal year 2026 and each subsequent fiscal year, such sums as may be necessary.

subsection.header: Technical assistance grants for nonprofit community-Based groups
subsection.text: Section 2064(b) of title 38, United States Code, is amended—

paragraph.text: by striking to carry out the program under this section. and inserting to carry out the program under this section—; and
by adding at the end the following new paragraphs:
$1,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2007 through 2012; and
for fiscal year 2026 and each subsequent fiscal year, such sums as may be necessary.

section.header: Permanent authority for certain programs for homeless veterans

subsection.header: General treatment for seriously mentally ill and homeless veterans
subsection.text: Section 2031 of title 38, United States Code, is amended—

subsection.header: Additional services for seriously ill mentally ill and homeless veterans at certain locations
subsection.text: Section 2033 of title 38, United States Code, is amended by striking subsection (d).
Section 2041 of title 38, United States Code, is amended by striking subsection (c).
Section 2066 of title 38, United States Code, is amended by striking subsection (d).
Section 8118(a) of title 38, United States Code, is amended by striking paragraph (5).
